sections.paragraph:
μυλών, ῶνος, ὁ (Eur. et al.; Thu. 6, 22; also Dio Chrys. 65 [15], 19; ins: BCH 27, 1903, 64, 146 [250 b.c.]; PCairMasp 139 p. 53, 13; Jer 52:11; on the form s. B-D-F §111, 5) mill-house (Ps.-Lucian, Asin. 42) Mt 24:41 v.l.—DELG s.v. μύλη A6.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 661
